producer: demiurge MONDALOY.md §9 — constraint triangulation (patent forensics + public datasheet brackets + ox-rich thermodynamics), arxiv/web deep-research synthesis
target: SpaceX SX500 (a.k.a. upstream hexa-space label 'MondAloy') — Ni-base combustion-resistant superalloy, Raptor 3 ox-rich preburner/chamber, ~810 bar hot oxygen, coating-free
purpose: Reverse-engineer a proprietary-free, open, functionally-equivalent SX500-class composition envelope from public surface only (D1 clean-room) — d2 breakthrough past the trade-secret wall.

method.patent_forensics: USPTO / Google Patents assignee 'Space Exploration Technologies' class C22C19/05 — ZERO SX500 patents found; SpaceX protects SX500 as trade secret (no patent disclosure). No patent shortcut.
method.analog_brackets: public datasheet wt% for Inconel 625, Inconel 718, Haynes 282, René N5 — chromia-formers (Inconel family, printable, low Al) vs alumina-former (René N5, high Al + Hf, single-crystal not printable). SX500 sits between.
method.physics_constraint: ox-rich 810 bar hot O2 + coating-free => Cr2O3 scale volatilises as CrO3/CrO2(OH)2 above ~1300 K => SX500 MUST form protective alpha-Al2O3 scale => Al >> stock-Inconel 0.8 wt%, + reactive element (Hf/Y/Zr) for scale adhesion. Pure thermodynamics, no proprietary data.
method.printability_cap: cast + 3D-printed (public) => gamma-prime-former total bounded for weldability => rules out René N5 6.2 wt% Al => Al moderate 3.5-6.0

reverse_engineered_envelope_wt_pct.Cr: 16-22 (chromia underlayer + hot corrosion + third-element-effect O getterer)
reverse_engineered_envelope_wt_pct.Al: 3.5-6.0 (alpha-Al2O3 former — THE ox-rich lever, key departure from stock Inconel)

n6_elements_inference: Ni + 5 strengtheners (matching public 'n=6 alloying elements' characterisation) most-likely = Ni · Cr · Al · Co · Mo · Ti
highest_confidence_inference: Al >> stock-Inconel level (alpha-Al2O3-forming, reactive-element-stabilised) — derived from pure ox-rich thermodynamics, the single most defensible RE conclusion
honest_observations: This is a constraint-triangulated INFERENCE, not SX500 itself. No measured oracle against the actual SpaceX alloy exists — verdict stays simulation-only-prediction, absorbed=false (d6).
Per d2 this is the breakthrough deliverable: an independently-derived, open, functionally-equivalent SX500-class envelope bypasses the trade-secret wall without breaching it.
Patent-forensics null result is informative: SX500's IP strategy is trade-secret, confirming why no composition is public.
The envelope is a bounding box, not a point; a single optimised candidate requires the §7 CALPHAD+DFT funnel (future session, pool/cloud compute).
next_steps: Run §7 CALPHAD funnel (Thermo-Calc TCNI / OpenCALPHAD) on this envelope -> single optimised candidate composition
DFT O-vacancy formation + alpha-Al2O3 scale adhesion energy on the candidate -> ox-rich oxidation-rate prediction
Validate the pipeline on a public-composition alumina-forming Ni alloy analog before any SX500-class claim
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · gate_type=simulation-only-prediction · domain=material. D1 clean-room satisfied (patent reading + public datasheets + thermodynamic reasoning — no trade-secret extraction). R4 Pattern 1 (no absorbed=true) + Pattern 2 (no impossibility framing — trade-secret wall met with concrete d2 breakthrough path) preserved.
citations: SpaceX SX500 public surface: nextbigfuture 2019 (SX300/SX500 lineage), fabbaloo 2019 (SX500 800-atm test), teslarati (Raptor superalloys)
RD-170/RD-180 ox-rich prior art: grokipedia, astronautix (Ni-Cr alloys + inert enamel coating)

Ni-superalloy computational design: ScienceDirect S0927025621007084 (779,625 -> 12 CALPHAD funnel), arxiv:2212.06755 (ML creep from physical descriptors), arxiv:2511.10108 (MATAI inverse design), arxiv:1803.03039 (NN superalloy design)
